content:
- All required lanes are green: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` passed after the new integrity contract and live-path proof were added.
- Marked story-12 task 01 as completed with `<passes>true</passes>`, updated the execution plan checklists, and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` so Ralph can move on.
- Next, if quitting immediately due to context limit: stage every changed file including `.ralph`, commit with the task-finished message including the test evidence, push, and then stop.

- Read the previous Codex progress log entry and the current Ralph active/done task summaries.
- Confirmed there are active failing tasks remaining after story-12 task 01 was completed; need to resolve blockers and priorities from the actual task files before choosing the next task.
- Next, inspect active task files plus the choose-task rules, then write the selected path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Inspected every active task file plus the choose-task rules and checked for priority/blocker/meta-task tags.
- Found no active failing bugs, no active blocker tags, no active explicit priorities, and no meta-task candidates; the repo is a Rust workspace centered on crates/runner and existing E2E integrity tests, so the next logical failing task is story 12 task 02.
- Next, overwrite .ralph/current_task.txt with .ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/02-task-assert-single-container-tls-and-scoped-role-integrity.md and stop immediately.

- Created .ralph/current_task.txt because it did not exist and set it to the chosen next task path.
- Selected story-12 task 02: no unblocked failing bugs were active, no blockers or priority tags changed ordering, and continuing the E2E integrity story is the most logical next step for the current Rust test architecture.
- Next, quit immediately so the next agent can execute .ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/02-task-assert-single-container-tls-and-scoped-role-integrity.md.

- Read the existing Codex progress log, confirmed the current task pointer, and loaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Current task points to story 12 task 02; next step is to inspect the task file and surrounding plan files to decide whether I must create a plan or execute one.
- If context ends now, resume by reading `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/02-task-assert-single-container-tls-and-scoped-role-integrity.md` and any plan file under its `_plans` directory.

- Read the task file and confirmed it does not yet point at an execution plan; this turn must create the plan and stop rather than implement.
- Began inspecting the previous story-12 plan and the current E2E/runtime code to identify the public behaviors to assert: single destination container, TLS HTTP, real CockroachDB, and a non-superuser scoped PostgreSQL role.
- If context ends now, continue by turning the observed runtime/test entry points into a plan file under the task `_plans` directory and update the task file to reference it.

- Confirmed the previous task's plan format and pulled the concrete E2E support/test files that likely bound this task.
- Need one more pass over `default_bootstrap_long_lane.rs`, `default_bootstrap_harness.rs`, and `e2e_harness.rs` to decide the smallest honest typed boundary and then write the new plan file.
- If context ends now, inspect those three files plus search task markdowns for how they reference plan state before editing `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/02...`.

- Determined that task 01 already introduced `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_integrity.rs`, so task 02 should likely extend that same typed integrity boundary instead of creating a second one.
- Reading the existing integrity contract, Docker/container contract, README/runtime contract, and role/TLS-related code paths to turn task 02 into a concrete execution plan.
- If context ends now, continue from `e2e_integrity.rs`, `e2e_integrity_contract.rs`, `Dockerfile`, and the destination-role/TLS references found by ripgrep.

- Confirmed the likely execution surfaces for task 02: E2E support (`e2e_harness.rs`, `default_bootstrap_harness.rs`, `e2e_integrity.rs`), fast integrity contract, plus container/README contract files.
- Need one last consistency check on how task markdowns point at plans, then I can write the task-02 plan with concrete TDD slices and stop.
- If context ends now, inspect comparable task markdowns in story 10/11 and then create `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/02-task-assert-single-container-tls-and-scoped-role-integrity_plans/...`.

- Verified that task files record the selected plan via a `<plan>` tag.
- Doing one final targeted read of the E2E harness ownership points: webhook sink selection, generated runner config, destination role/database preparation, and the runner image harness that may carry the single-container contract.
- If context ends now, use those reads to write the task-02 plan and update the task markdown with the new plan path.

- Created the task-02 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-12-verify-e2e-integrity/02-task-assert-single-container-tls-and-scoped-role-integrity_plans/2026-04-19-single-container-tls-scoped-role-integrity-plan.md` and linked it from the task markdown via `<plan>`.
- The plan uses the existing `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` direction, extends the typed `e2e_integrity.rs` boundary, and calls out the likely core refactor: moving the honest E2E path to one containerized runner runtime instead of the current host-process shortcut.
- Next, quit immediately. The next turn should read the task file, see the linked plan ending in `NOW EXECUTE`, then execute the slices and drive `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` to green.

- Read the Ralph progress log, current task pointer, and required `tdd` / `improve-code-boundaries` skill files.
- Confirmed the current task is story-12 task 02 and the task markdown already links to a dedicated plan file.
- Next, inspect the plan state (`TO BE VERIFIED` vs `NOW EXECUTE`) and then either execute the red/green slices or stop immediately if the design must be revisited.

- Added the first fast RED/GREEN slice for task 02: `e2e_integrity_contract.rs` now requires a typed `RuntimeShapeAudit`, and `DefaultBootstrapHarness` / the default long-lane test route runtime-shape assertions through it.
- Ran the targeted fast contract to RED then GREEN. Ran the first ignored long-lane default-bootstrap test to drive the next RED; after fixing a real `rolsuper::text` parser bug (`false` vs `f`), the test now fails for the intended reason: the default path still reports `HostProcess` instead of `SingleContainer`.
- Next, refactor the default E2E harness runtime ownership so the honest path starts one runner container from the Docker image, then re-run the same ignored long-lane test until the runtime-shape audit passes.

- Switched the default bootstrap harness onto a containerized runner runtime and introduced a reusable `RunnerContainerProcess` so runtime-shape evidence can see one runner container, HTTPS health, and container-network Postgres targeting.
- Removed the nested `cargo build -p source-bootstrap` anti-pattern from runner tests by calling the `source-bootstrap` library directly; this fixed the hidden Cargo lock hang that was blocking the long-lane test loop.
- Verified the key ignored long-lane bootstrap scenario now passes with the new runtime-shape audit. Next: run the broader required lanes (`make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`), fix fallout, then update task/plan checkboxes when everything is green.
